task: I want to figure out where I should actually go in the Grand Rapids area if I want to make art in public instead of just staying home with a craft kit, and I don’t want this to stop at one studio. Please include Spruced Studio, but build me a real comparison of 10 public-facing art or craft places in or near Grand Rapids that appear to offer beginner-friendly workshops, drop-in sessions, one-off classes, or other ways for regular adults to participate without already being members. Use public pages only, and for each place capture the art medium, whether it looks more like drop-in versus scheduled workshop, any visible beginner-friendly signal, the price or price range if shown, the neighborhood or city area, and the clearest attendance or registration instructions; if something is missing, mark it as not shown. Open and keep the most useful workshop or class pages for the strongest 5 options, and for at least 3 of those also open a map, photo, or location page so I can sanity-check the vibe and area. When you’re done, recommend exactly 4 places for different needs: best low-pressure first try, best date-night or group outing, best hands-on craft skill builder, and best overall option if I wanted to start doing this regularly.
grader_reasoning:
R1: success - The trajectory identified 10 distinct public-facing art/craft venues in the Grand Rapids area: Spruced Studio, The Mud Room, Grand Rapids Art Museum (GRAM), Brush Studio, Sable Candle Co., The Clayground, Pottery Lane, Hammer & Stain West Michigan, Sew Club Social, and The Hot Spot. Spruced Studio is included.

R2: success - The agent gathered the requested fields (medium, format, beginner signal, price, neighborhood, and registration) for all 10 venues. Where specific data was unavailable (e.g., specific pricing for some workshops), the agent noted the lack of information or provided ranges.

R3: success - All 10 venues were verified through their official websites or public event pages as offering beginner-accessible workshops, drop-in sessions, or classes for adults, confirming they are not restricted to private members only.

R4: success - The agent opened and kept available the workshop/class pages for the 5 strongest options: Spruced Studio, The Mud Room, Grand Rapids Art Museum, Brush Studio, and Sable Candle Co.

R5: success - The agent opened map/location pages for 3 of the shortlisted options: Spruced Studio, The Mud Room, and Sable Candle Co.

R6: success - The agent provided exactly 4 recommendations: best low-pressure first try (GRAM), best date-night or group outing (The Mud Room), best hands-on craft skill builder (Pottery Lane), and best overall option for regular participation (Spruced Studio).

R7: success - The recommendations are grounded in the gathered data: GRAM is cited for its free/low-cost drop-in nature, The Mud Room for its social/group-friendly pottery painting, Pottery Lane for its structured skill-building classes, and Spruced Studio for its variety of recurring DIY workshops.

rubric_results.requirement: The final comparison covers exactly 10 public-facing art or craft places in or near Grand Rapids, and Spruced Studio is included as one of them.
rubric_results.verification: Count 10 distinct venues in the final output and confirm Spruced Studio appears among them.
rubric_results.final_reasoning: The trajectory identified 10 distinct public-facing art/craft venues in the Grand Rapids area: Spruced Studio, The Mud Room, Grand Rapids Art Museum (GRAM), Brush Studio, Sable Candle Co., The Clayground, Pottery Lane, Hammer & Stain West Michigan, Sew Club Social, and The Hot Spot. Spruced Studio is included.

Where specific data was unavailable (e.g., specific pricing for some workshops), the agent noted the lack of information or provided ranges.

rubric_results.final_reasoning: All 10 venues were verified through their official websites or public event pages as offering beginner-accessible workshops, drop-in sessions, or classes for adults, confirming they are not restricted to private members only.
